worked: **What didn't**: missing `"type": "basic"` on a Karabiner manipulator silently broke the whole profile (the simple_modifications for Nums 1–3 stopped firing as a side effect — Karabiner validates at profile level, not rule level). First osascript used `make new ... with properties {target:...}` — Finder silently drops the `target` property, opens at root.

**Gotchas**: `plutil -convert json` refuses on plists containing `<date>` or `<data>` (lossy). macOS "switch to a Space with open windows" setting can override AppleScript intent unless windows are created before activate. Karabiner profile validation is all-or-nothing.
Memory file `project_mac_migration.md` is the durable migration record — read it before re-deriving state.
`~/mac-migration/AGENT-HANDOFF.md` (on both Macs) is the cross-machine handoff doc; `HANDOFF-RESPONSE-i-dream.md` is the most recent inter-agent exchange.
Reverse SSH is wired (old Mac sshd ON, new-Mac key in `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`).
Don't re-narrate the migration history; act on the small remaining list.

feedback: **What worked**: mtime comparison as a single-shot discriminator between rsync and clone (decisive in the i-dream diagnosis). Two-step osascript for new Finder window. Plist binary→XML via `plutil` for diffable git. Per-tool `restore.sh` encoding gotchas (cfprefsd reload after plist drop).

**What didn't**: missing `"type": "basic"` on a Karabiner manipulator silently broke the whole profile (the simple_modifications for Nums 1–3 stopped firing as a side effect — Karabiner validates at profile level, not rule level). First osascript used `make new ... with properties {target:...}` — Finder silently drops the `target` property, opens at root.

**Gotchas**: `plutil -convert json` refuses on plists containing `<date>` or `<data>` (lossy). macOS "switch to a Space with open windows" setting can override AppleScript intent unless windows are created before activate. Karabiner profile validation is all-or-nothing.
Memory file `project_mac_migration.md` is the durable migration record — read it before re-deriving state.
`~/mac-migration/AGENT-HANDOFF.md` (on both Macs) is the cross-machine handoff doc; `HANDOFF-RESPONSE-i-dream.md` is the most recent inter-agent exchange.
Reverse SSH is wired (old Mac sshd ON, new-Mac key in `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`).
Don't re-narrate the migration history; act on the small remaining list.
